overall settings.title: Bacon for Dinner — Walkthrough
overall settings.description:
If you felt a bit lost in the first scenario, consider going through this guided tour to get more out of the game. Same story but with detailed explanations, in lieu of a help section.

Begin by clicking on the **Home** location.

attribs.name: Climbing
attribs.description: Often easier for kids than adults.

attribs.name: Pays for Odd Jobs
attribs.description: Has money to pay for chores.

items.name: Branch
items.description: Better for fending off animals than a knife.

items.name: Cash
items.description:
Move Suzy to the **Farmhouse** get your earnings. Navigate to **Uncle** > **Pays for Odd Jobs** > **Cash.**

Getting paid doesn't take any particular amount of time, so there's a *Gain Instant* button instead of *Start,* but otherwise, it works the same. The default is *Max* to add up the earnings all at once. Now, with the cash, have Uncle *give to* Suzy.

Since you don't have enough cash to buy a pig yet, go back to **Chores** and repeat this process. Once you do have enough, go to **Uncle's Farm** and buy the pigs, placing them at that location. Of course, you'll need more than one pig in order to get baby pigs.

items.name: Chores
items.description:
**Chores** is a *universal* item (or *general storage*), meaning that it can be used anywhere and isn't carried by a specific person. In this case, it's a way to remember how much work Suzy has done.

Press *Start* to do some chores. There's no limit to the chores that can be done, but you can only get paid for 10 at a time.

items.name: Cooked Bacon
items.description:
"`0 / 20`" means that although there are none yet, we can have up to 20 at a time.

Looks like crafting requires 1 **Raw Bacon,** but we do not start with any in stock. Where could that be found? Keep an eye out for possible sources as you investigate and travel.

We'll also need the **Wood Stove** to cook the bacon. Since **Suzy** is currently at **Home** and we viewed her page recently, the wood stove should show *Have 1,* indicating that it's available.

items.name: Knife
items.description: For butchering. Not much of a weapon, at least not in the hands of a young girl.

items.name: Phone
items.description: Suzy's mother is the first number on the contacts list.

items.name: Pigs
items.description:
The most efficient plan would be to raise more pigs after butchering only 2 of them, by pressing *Start* on the row where pigs are the *Source* (as well as the result). However, let's do it the hard way this time. Go to **Raw Bacon** and slaughter all of them!

After you've dispatched the original herd, it's time to earn some cash to buy more. Navigate to **Cash** > **Chores.**

When you've bought a new pair of pigs, use them to raise more. Then, finish the stack of **Raw Bacon,** leaving at least a pair left.

items.name: Raw Bacon
items.description:
Now we can make use of that **Knife** Suzy has been carrying. There are 4 pigs currently at the farm. Press *Start* and wait while she turns them into neatly sized pieces of meat.

At the top, "Carried by Suzy" indicates whose inventory the bacon will go to, and who is holding the knife. If it doesn't say that, you may need to go back to her screen and click on the knife to get to this screen. Similarly, if the pigs are at the farm but it says "Have 0" here, navigate back to the farm and then click through the pigs to get here.

Looks like we ran out. Go back to **Pigs** and see how to get more.

Once you have a full load of raw bacon (20), it's time for Suzy to start the perilous trip back home. Navigate to her page to return to **Sketchy-Path Forest.**

items.name: Table
items.description: At home, drop cooked bacon to go on the table.

items.name: Wood Stove
items.description: This item functions as a crafting station, fixed at the **Home** location. Useful for producing items from recipes, namely, **Cooked Bacon** — go ahead and click on it.

locations.name: Farmhouse
locations.description: Board siding and coruscated metal roof — primitive construction.

locations.name: Home
locations.description:
First, take a look around. Click on her **Mom** and then **Suzy** to see that both have **Pathfinding,** but Suzy's is lower. This means she'll be more likely to get lost in the woods if her Mom isn't there.

Also, open the **Wood Stove** link in a new browser window or tab (often convenient in this game). After reading about **Cooked Bacon,** close to return to this page.

Next, it's time to explore. The only destination we can get to from home shows *(not yet visited)*, so we don't know anything about it yet. Bring up Suzy's page again to travel.

locations.name: Sketchy-Path Forest
locations.description:
To cross this area, **Suzy** is expected to run the **Navigate Woods** event. However, let's assume everything goes smoothly during the daytime, that is, the first time Suzy visits. Use the numpad (or arrow keys; navigation buttons on mobile) to move to the eastern exit. Then press "t" to travel, or click on Suzy and select the destination.

* * * * *

On the return trip, go to **Suzy** > **Navigate Woods.**

locations.name: Uncle's Farm
locations.description: Hopefully, you immediately recognized that **Pigs** mean *bacon*, which is what we're after. Click on them to continue.

characters.name: Mom
characters.description:
An NPC. You can still control her actions, though, if she is needed in the woods later to rescue Suzy.

After viewing this character the first time, return **Home.** After viewing this character the first time, return **Home.** The _{subscript} or bracketed letter indicates a hotkey (not shown on mobile).

characters.name: Suzy
characters.description:
This is your player character for the scenario. In the upper right menu, find *User Settings* and change username to "Playing as Suzy" (or another name you prefer). Then return **Home.**

Once you're finished at **Home** and are ready to travel, take a look at the possible destinations to travel to. There's only one, initially shown as *(not yet visited),* so select it and press *Go*. On arrival, the location is revealed.

characters.name: Uncle
characters.description: Will pay a modest salary for chores.

characters.name: Wolf
characters.description:
Shaggy forest dweller with teeth and a snarl.

To appear, set location to **Sketchy-Path Forest** and grid position, for example `4,1`.

events.name: Connect to Service
events.description:
Suzy needs to call her **Mother** for help of some kind. Choose *Easy* difficulty since we won't be far from civilization, then roll.

On *Major Success,* Mother explains to Suzy how to get home, meaning that she will arrive home without further incident. On *Minor Success,* Mother can travel to the woods and lead Suzy out, using her higher **Pathfinding** skill so that **Navigate Woods** has a better chance to succeed.

On a single *Minor Failure,* Suzy can probably just try to call again (wouldn't you, if you were her?) But if things get worse, go to setup and put the **Wolf** character at this location, then roll **Contest Enemy.** Avoiding the wolf might still be the best plan, but if push comes to shove, she can grab a fallen branch to fend it off, or climb a tree, or yell.

This is the end of the walkthrough. After returning home, Suzy can cook the bacon. Once the win conditions in *Scenario Settings* are met, go to the overview page to see that the scenario has been completed.

events.name: Contest Enemy
events.description:
Run, hide, frighten, fight—the choice is up to you.

Edit this event to add **Climbing** as a *Determinant.* Or, [create an attribute](/configure/#attrib), such as **Strength** for fighting, and assign Suzy a value (she would be weak).

events.name: Navigate Woods
events.description:
Because Suzy is tired after all those chores, set difficulty to *Hard,* meaning there is a substantial risk of of getting lost or eaten by wolves.

For this walkthrough, keep rolling until the outcome is a *Minor Failure.* We'll take that to mean she's lost but there are no wolves or twisted ankles.

To get out of this pickle, go to Suzy's page and and find the **Connect to Service** event.
